Problem Statement: Maritime Email Extraction & Cost Optimization System
Project Background
In the shipbroking and chartering industry, brokers and charterers receive thousands of emails daily containing cargo requirements, vessel availability, voyage charter details, and time charter information.
These emails are generally unstructured and contain operational details in multiple formats, abbreviations, and free-text maritime terminology.
Currently, we use Large Language Model (LLM) APIs to extract structured information from these emails. While extraction accuracy is acceptable, the operational cost is extremely high due to:
Large email volume
Long email content
Multiple extraction calls per email
Continuous daily processing
Repeated parsing of similar formats
We want to significantly reduce or eliminate dependency on expensive LLM APIs while maintaining high extraction accuracy.
The goal is to design and implement a scalable, intelligent, low-cost extraction architecture.

Current Extraction Scope
The system currently extracts the following structured data.
Source reference:

1. Cargo VC Extraction (Voyage Charter)
Fields:
email_type
cargo_name
account_name
cargo_type
min_size
max_size
region
matching_region
load_port
discharge_port
laycan_start_date
laycan_end_date
email_id
phone_number
latitude
longitude
restriction
reason


2. Cargo TC Extraction (Time Charter)
Fields:
email_type
cargo_name
account_name
cargo_type
min_size
max_size
region
matching_region
del_port
redel_port
laycan_start_date
laycan_end_date
duration
email_id
phone_number
latitude
longitude
restriction
reason


3. Tonnage Extraction
Fields:
email_type
tonnage_name
tonnage_type
region
matching_region
port
open_date
close_date
dwt
pic
email_id
phone_number
latitude
longitude
restriction
reason


Additional Business Rules
Source reference:
Rules include:
Convert all dates to YYYY-MM-DD
If laycan_end missing → laycan_start + 5 days
If duration range given → use max value
If duration unit missing → assume days
Convert quantities to metric tons
Extract ALL entries from email
Extract PIC, email, phone from signature
Port format standardization:
Port, Country



Maritime Classification Standards
Cargo Type Values
Dry Bulk
General Cargo
Crude Oil
Chemical
Gas

Tonnage Type Values
General cargo
Bulk Carrier
Crude oil tanker
Chemical tanker
Gas Carrier

Vessel Size Conversion Rules
Handymax   = 10000 - 49999
Supramax   = 50000 - 59999
Ultramax   = 60000 - 69999
Panamax    = 70000 - 79999
Kamsarmax  = 80000 - 89999
Baby Cape  = 90000 - 199999
Capesize   = 200000+


Existing Challenges
1. Extremely High LLM API Cost
Current extraction heavily relies on external LLM APIs.
Problems:
High token consumption
Long maritime emails
Multiple extraction retries
Thousands of emails daily
High recurring monthly cost

2. Repetitive Email Patterns
Most maritime emails follow repetitive formats:
Fixed broker templates
Standard abbreviations
Predictable field locations
Common voyage terminology
Repeated vessel descriptions
Using expensive LLM inference for every email is inefficient.

3. Multi-Entry Parsing Complexity
One email may contain:
Multiple cargoes
Multiple vessels
Mixed VC and TC requirements
Signature blocks
Operational clauses
System must split and extract all entries correctly.

Objective
Design a hybrid extraction architecture that:
Reduces LLM API usage by 70–95%
Maintains high extraction accuracy
Supports maritime abbreviations and formats
Handles bulk email processing
Is scalable and production-ready

Expected Solution Approach
The developer is expected to propose and implement a cost-optimized architecture.
Possible approaches may include:

1. Rule-Based Extraction Layer
Use:
Regex
Pattern matching
Maritime dictionaries
NLP tokenization
Template detection
Examples:
LAYCAN:
DWT:
LP:
DP:
DEL:
REDEL:

This layer should extract straightforward fields without LLM calls.

2. Template Classification Engine
Detect recurring broker templates and process them using predefined parsers.
Example:
Broker A format
Broker B format
SeaSchiffe format
YB Global Shipping format

Goal:
Avoid LLM calls for known formats
Improve speed
Reduce cost

3. Lightweight ML / Local NLP Models
Possible options:
spaCy NER
DistilBERT
Small local transformer models
Fine-tuned maritime extraction model
Goal:
Run locally
Avoid per-token API cost
Improve entity extraction

4. Hybrid Confidence-Based Pipeline
Suggested flow:
Step 1:
Rule Engine

Step 2:
Template Parser

Step 3:
Local ML Model

Step 4:
Fallback to LLM only if confidence is low

This ensures LLM is used only for difficult emails.

5. Maritime Knowledge Base
Create dictionaries for:
Ports
Regions
Cargo names
Vessel types
Maritime abbreviations
Country mapping
Example:
ECI = East Coast India
WAFR = West Africa
UMX = Ultramax
SMX = Supramax


6. Signature Parsing Engine
Extract:
PIC
Email
Phone Number
from email signatures separately using regex and heuristics.

7. Email Segmentation Engine
Split emails into:
Cargo blocks
Vessel blocks
Signature blocks
Operational notes
before extraction.

Success Metrics
Primary Goals
Reduce LLM API cost by minimum 70%
Achieve extraction accuracy above 90%
Process emails in near real-time
Support batch processing

Technical Expectations
Developer should provide:
Architecture Design
Processing pipeline
Model selection
Parsing strategy
Confidence scoring
Scalability Plan
Queue processing
Parallel extraction
Caching strategy
Cost Optimization Strategy
Local inference
Rule-first architecture
Smart LLM fallback logic
Evaluation Metrics
Precision
Recall
F1 score
Cost per email
Processing latency

Sample Input Data
Large collection of real maritime emails containing:
Voyage charter cargoes
Time charter requirements
Tonnage positions
Vessel specifications
Examples available in attached extraction document.

Expected Deliverables
Developer should provide:
1. System Architecture
Detailed extraction flow.
2. Parsing Engine
Rule-based + ML implementation.
3. Training / Mapping Logic
Maritime dictionaries and normalization.
4. Cost Analysis
Current vs proposed architecture.
5. Benchmark Report
Accuracy and performance comparison.
6. Deployment Plan
Cloud/local deployment recommendations.

Final Goal
Build an intelligent maritime email extraction platform that minimizes dependency on expensive LLM APIs while maintaining high-quality structured extraction for chartering and shipbroking operations.

arguments.content:
openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  # Do not change the title, if the title changes, the import paths will be broken
  title: Api
  version: 0.1.0
  description: Maritime Email Extraction API
servers:
  - url: /api
    description: Base API path
tags:
  - name: health
    description: Health operations
  - name: extraction
    description: Email extraction operations
  - name: history
    description: Extraction history
  - name: stats
    description: Cost and performance statistics
  - name: knowledge
    description: Maritime knowledge base
paths:
  /healthz:
    get:
      operationId: healthCheck
      tags: [health]
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/HealthStatus"

  /emails/extract:
    post:
      operationId: extractEmail
      tags: [extraction]
      summary: Extract structured data from a maritime email
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/ExtractionInput"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Extraction result
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ExtractionJob"
        "400":
          description: Bad request

  /emails:
    get:
      operationId: listExtractions
      tags: [history]
      summary: List past extraction jobs
      parameters:
        - name: limit
          in: query
          schema:
            type: integer
            default: 20
        - name: offset
          in: query
          schema:
            type: integer
            default: 0
        - name: emailType
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [VC, TC, Tonnage, Mixed, Unknown]
      responses:
        "200":
          description: List of extraction jobs
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ExtractionListResponse"

  /emails/{id}:
    get:
      operationId: getExtraction
      tags: [history]
      summary: Get a single extraction job
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: integer
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Extraction job details
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ExtractionJob"
        "404":
          description: Not found

    delete:
      operationId: deleteExtraction
      tags: [history]
      summary: Delete an extraction job
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: integer
      responses:
        "204":
          description: Deleted

  /stats:
    get:
      operationId: getStats
      tags: [stats]
      summary: Get cost savings and extraction statistics
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Platform statistics
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/PlatformStats"

  /knowledge/regions:
    get:
      operationId: listRegions
      tags: [knowledge]
      summary: List maritime region abbreviations
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Regions list
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: "#/components/schemas/KnowledgeEntry"

  /knowledge/ports:
    get:
      operationId: listPorts
      tags: [knowledge]
      summary: List known maritime ports
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Ports list
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: "#/components/schemas/KnowledgeEntry"

  /knowledge/vessel-sizes:
    get:
      operationId: listVesselSizes
      tags: [knowledge]
      summary: List vessel size classifications
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Vessel sizes
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: "#/components/schemas/VesselSizeEntry"

components:
  schemas:
    HealthStatus:
      type: object
      properties:
        status:
          type: string
      required:
        - status

    ExtractionInput:
      type: object
      required:
        - emailText
      properties:
        emailText:
          type: string
          minLength: 10
        subject:
          type: ["string", "null"]

    ExtractionJob:
      type: object
      required:
        - id
        - emailText
        - emailType
        - pipeline
        - extractedEntries
        - processingMs
        - llmUsed
        - estimatedCostUsd
        - createdAt
      properties:
        id:
          type: integer
        emailText:
          type: string
        subject:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        emailType:
          type: string
          enum: [VC, TC, Tonnage, Mixed, Unknown]
        pipeline:
          type: string
          enum: [rule-based, template, llm-fallback]
          description: Which pipeline layer handled this email
        confidence:
          type: number
          description: Confidence score 0-1
        extractedEntries:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/ExtractedEntry"
        processingMs:
          type: integer
        llmUsed:
          type: boolean
        estimatedCostUsd:
          type: number
        createdAt:
          type: string

    ExtractedEntry:
      type: object
      required:
        - entryType
        - fields
        - confidence
        - extractionMethod
      properties:
        entryType:
          type: string
          enum: [VC, TC, Tonnage]
        confidence:
          type: number
        extractionMethod:
          type: string
          enum: [rule-based, template, llm-fallback]
        fields:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/ExtractedFields"

    ExtractedFields:
      type: object
      properties:
        email_type:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        cargo_name:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        account_name:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        cargo_type:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        tonnage_name:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        tonnage_type:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        min_size:
          type: ["number", "null"]
        max_size:
          type: ["number", "null"]
        region:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        matching_region:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        load_port:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        discharge_port:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        del_port:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        redel_port:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        port:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        open_date:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        close_date:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        laycan_start_date:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        laycan_end_date:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        duration:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        dwt:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        pic:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        email_id:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        phone_number:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        restriction:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        reason:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        latitude:
          type: ["number", "null"]
        longitude:
          type: ["number", "null"]

    ExtractionListResponse:
      type: object
      required:
        - items
        - total
      properties:
        items:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/ExtractionJob"
        total:
          type: integer

    PlatformStats:
      type: object
      required:
        - totalEmails
        - ruleBasedCount
        - templateCount
        - llmFallbackCount
        - estimatedLlmSavingsUsd
        - avgProcessingMs
        - avgConfidence
        - costReductionPct
        - recentActivity
      properties:
        totalEmails:
          type: integer
        ruleBasedCount:
          type: integer
        templateCount:
          type: integer
        llmFallbackCount:
          type: integer
        estimatedLlmSavingsUsd:
          type: number
        avgProcessingMs:
          type: number
        avgConfidence:
          type: number
        costReductionPct:
          type: number
        recentActivity:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/ActivityPoint"

    ActivityPoint:
      type: object
      required:
        - date
        - count
        - llmUsed
      properties:
        date:
          type: string
        count:
          type: integer
        llmUsed:
          type: integer

    KnowledgeEntry:
      type: object
      required:
        - code
        - fullName
        - category
      properties:
        code:
          type: string
        fullName:
          type: string
        category:
          type: string

    VesselSizeEntry:
      type: object
      required:
        - name
        - minDwt
        - maxDwt
      properties:
        name:
          type: string
        minDwt:
          type: integer
        maxDwt:
          type: ["integer", "null"]
        description:
          type: ["string", "null"]

     1→## Frontend
     2→
     3→- Use `wouter` for routing on the frontend.
     4→  - If you need to add a new page, add them to the `src/pages` directory and register them in `src/App.tsx`.
     5→  - If there are multiple pages, use a sidebar for navigation. Use the `Link` component or the `useLocation` hook from `wouter` instead of modifying the window directly.
     6→- For forms, always use shadcn's `useForm` hook and `Form` component from `@/components/ui/form` which wraps `react-hook-form`.
     7→  - When appropriate, use the `zodResolver` from `@hookform/resolvers/zod` to validate form data.
     8→  - Use `.extend` to add validation rules to the schema.
     9→  - Remember that the form component is controlled, ensure you pass default values to the `useForm` hook.
    10→- Use the Orval-generated hooks from `@workspace/api-client-react` for all API calls. Do not write raw `fetch` or manual TanStack Query hooks when generated hooks exist.
    11→  - Generated query hooks return `T` directly — NOT wrapped in `{ data: T }`.
    12→  - When passing options like `enabled`, you MUST also pass `queryKey`:
    13→
    14→    ```typescript
    15→    const { data } = useGetNote(noteId, { query: { enabled: !!noteId, queryKey: getGetNoteQueryKey(noteId) } });
    16→    ```
    17→
    18→  - For mutations, `onSuccess` receives `T` directly:
    19→
    20→    ```typescript
    21→    createRoom.mutate({ data: { name } }, {
    22→      onSuccess: (room) => { setLocation(`/room/${room.id}`); }
    23→    });
    24→    ```
    25→
    26→  - Invalidate related queries after mutations using the generated queryKey helpers.
    27→  - Show a loading or skeleton state while queries (via `.isLoading`) or mutations (via `.isPending`) are being made.
    28→- Common pitfalls to avoid:
    29→  - The `useToast` hook is exported from `@/hooks/use-toast`.
    30→  - If a form is failing to submit, try logging out `form.formState.errors` to see if there are form validation errors for fields that might not have associated form fields.
    31→  - DO NOT explicitly import React as the existing Vite setup has a JSX transformer that does it automatically.
    32→  - Use `import.meta.env.<ENV_VAR>` to access environment variables on the frontend instead of `process.env.<ENV_VAR>`. Note that variables must be prefixed with `VITE_` in order for the env vars to be available on the frontend.
    33→  - `<SelectItem>` will throw an error if it has no value prop. Provide a value prop like this `<SelectItem value="option1">`.
    34→  - NEVER import something you don't use — TypeScript will fail the build on unused imports.
    35→  - Always import React hooks (`useState`, `useEffect`, `useRef`, `useCallback`, `useMemo`) from `"react"` — never from a router library like `"wouter"`.
    36→- Add a `data-testid` attribute to every HTML element that users can interact with (buttons, inputs, links, etc.) and to elements displaying meaningful information (user data, status messages, dynamic content, key values).
    37→  - Use unique, descriptive identifiers following this pattern:
    38→    - Interactive elements: `{action}-{target}` (e.g., `button-submit`, `input-email`, `link-profile`)
    39→    - Display elements: `{type}-{content}` (e.g., `text-username`, `img-avatar`, `status-payment`)
    40→  - For dynamically generated elements (lists, grids, repeated components), append a unique identifier at the end: `{type}-{description}-{id}`
    41→    - Examples: `card-product-${productId}`, `row-user-${index}`, `text-price-${itemId}`
    42→    - The dynamic identifier can be any unique value (database ID, index, key) as long as it's unique within that group

await startAsyncSubagent({
    specialization: "DESIGN",
    task: `Build the full frontend for a Maritime Email Extraction platform. This is a professional operations tool used by shipbrokers and chartering teams to extract structured data from maritime emails.

Product identity: A precision-grade extraction terminal for maritime professionals — feels like Bloomberg for shipbrokers. Dense, authoritative, built for people who live and breathe freight markets. Every second saved is money earned.

Vibe: Tight, fast, and information-rich — like a trading desk where every pixel earns its place. Professional dark blues and slate tones with amber accent for cost savings / warnings. Dense but scannable.

Pages (use wouter routing, base at "/"):
- "/" (Dashboard) — platform stats overview: total emails processed, cost reduction %, pipeline breakdown (rule-based vs template vs LLM fallback), avg confidence, recent activity chart using recharts AreaChart
- "/extract" (Extract Email) — main feature: large textarea for pasting email, submit button, then show extraction results. Results display: each extracted entry as a card with all fields in a structured table, show which pipeline handled it (badge: rule-based = green, template = blue, llm-fallback = orange), show confidence score, show cost estimate
- "/history" (History) — paginated table of past extractions with columns: date, type badge (VC/TC/Tonnage/Mixed), pipeline badge, entries count, confidence, processing time, cost estimate, delete action
- "/history/:id" (Extraction Detail) — full detail view of a single extraction, showing original email text + all extracted entries with all fields
- "/knowledge" (Knowledge Base) — three tabs: Regions (code → full name), Ports (code → full name), Vessel Sizes (name, DWT range). Display as searchable tables

Data types:
- ExtractionJob: { id, emailText, subject, emailType (VC|TC|Tonnage|Mixed|Unknown), pipeline (rule-based|template|llm-fallback), confidence, extractedEntries[], processingMs, llmUsed, estimatedCostUsd, createdAt }
- ExtractedEntry: { entryType (VC|TC|Tonnage), confidence, extractionMethod, fields: { email_type, cargo_name, account_name, cargo_type, tonnage_name, tonnage_type, min_size, max_size, region, load_port, discharge_port, del_port, redel_port, port, open_date, close_date, laycan_start_date, laycan_end_date, duration, dwt, pic, email_id, phone_number, restriction } }
- PlatformStats: { totalEmails, ruleBasedCount, templateCount, llmFallbackCount, estimatedLlmSavingsUsd, avgProcessingMs, avgConfidence, costReductionPct, recentActivity: [{date, count, llmUsed}] }

Available API hooks (import from "@workspace/api-client-react"):
- useExtractEmail() — mutation, POST /api/emails/extract, body: { emailText, subject? }
- useListExtractions(params?, options?) — query, GET /api/emails, params: { limit?, offset?, emailType? }
- useGetExtraction(id, options?) — query, GET /api/emails/:id
- useDeleteExtraction() — mutation, DELETE /api/emails/:id
- useGetStats(options?) — query, GET /api/stats
- useListRegions(options?) — query, GET /api/knowledge/regions
- useListPorts(options?) — query, GET /api/knowledge/ports
- useListVesselSizes(options?) — query, GET /api/knowledge/vessel-sizes
- getListExtractionsQueryKey(params?) — queryKey helper
- getGetExtractionQueryKey(id) — queryKey helper
- getGetStatsQueryKey() — queryKey helper

Hook integration rules:
- Import hooks from "@workspace/api-client-react", never from relative paths
- Create all new files BEFORE importing them
- Hooks return T directly (not wrapped)
- Query hook options: useGetExtraction(id, { query: { enabled: !!id, queryKey: getGetExtractionQueryKey(id) } })
- Only import what you use — unused imports break TypeScript builds
- Do not use emojis anywhere in the UI
- Use ALL the provided hooks

Use recharts AreaChart for the recent activity chart on the dashboard. Use lucide-react icons throughout.

Supporting screens (sidebar navigation, 404, loading states, error states) deserve the same creative energy as the main app.

Prioritize clarity and polish — but this should feel like a product someone chose, not a tool they were assigned. Users spend hours here; reward that with thoughtful details. Make a bold, deliberate color choice that someone would remember after closing the tab.`,

     6→## Overview
     7→
     8→A workflow binds a shell command (e.g., `npm run dev`, `python run.py`, `cargo run`) to a long-running task managed by Replit. Workflows are used to run webservers, background services, TUIs, and other persistent processes.
     9→
    10→**Key characteristics:**
    11→
    12→- Workflows run until explicitly stopped
    13→- The system tracks workflows automatically — no separate configuration file is required
    14→- Workflows auto-restart after package installation and module installation
    15→- You can only get console logs from a workflow if it is running
    16→
    17→## Setup Tips
    18→
    19→1. **Keep workflows minimal**: One workflow per project is usually sufficient. Use the main frontend server or TUI as your primary workflow.

    21→2. **Choose the right workflow**: If your project has a frontend or TUI, set the workflow to the process that updates what the user sees.
    22→
    23→3. **Clean up unused workflows**: When adding new workflows, remove any existing workflows that are no longer needed.
    24→
    25→4. **Always restart after changes**: Restart workflows after making server-side code changes to ensure updates are visible to the user. Verify they run without errors before returning to the user.
    26→
    27→5. **Use bash for one-off commands**: Workflows are for persistent processes. Use bash for build scripts, testing, or commands that don't need to keep running.

    81→### createArtifact(artifactType, slug, previewPath, title)
    82→
    83→Bootstrap and register a new artifact in one call. Default for all new artifacts; requires an unused `slug`.

    86→
    87→<!-- BEGIN_ARTIFACT_LIST -->

   205→<!-- END_SERVICES_TABLE -->
   206→
   207→The web service's URL prefix is set to whatever you pass as `previewPath`. Route handling is prefix-aware: frontend routes and API requests must include this prefix.
   208→
   209→**Mobile:** Expo is served at its assigned port and uses `previewPath` as its registered route.
   210→
   211→## Failure Recovery
   212→
   213→If `createArtifact` fails, inspect the error and retry with corrected inputs. The callback requires a clean `slug` on each attempt, so remove any partial `artifacts/<slug>/` directory before reusing that slug.
   214→
   215→- **Slug already exists** → Choose a different `slug`, or remove the existing artifact directory before retrying
   216→- **`DUPLICATE_PREVIEW_PATH`** → Choose a different `previewPath`
   217→- **Bootstrap fails** → Fix the reported shell/setup issue, then retry with a clean slug
   218→- **Artifact is missing `files/`** → Migrate that artifact type to the shared bootstrap layout before using `createArtifact`
   219→
   220→## Examples
   221→
   222→<!-- BEGIN_EXAMPLES -->
   223→### Mobile app (`expo`)
   224→
   225→```javascript
   226→const result = await createArtifact({
   227→    artifactType: "expo",
   228→    slug: "my-app",
   229→    previewPath: "/",
   230→    title: "My App"
   231→});
   232→const expoPort = result.ports.expo;
   233→
   234→// Expo is scaffolded under artifacts/my-app
   235→// and API work should be added to the shared api-server.
   236→//
   237→// Multi-artifact note: do not tell the subagent to read a sibling web
   238→// artifact's src/index.css for design tokens here — that CSS is only
   239→// trustworthy after the web frontend build has finished. Instead,
   240→// extract tokens in the main loop after the web build completes, sync
   241→// them into constants/colors.ts (colors + radius), app.json, and _layout.tsx yourself,
   242→// then launch the Expo subagent with the synced files. See
   243→// multi-artifact-creation.md "Visual Consistency" for the full sequence.
   244→await startAsyncSubagent({
   245→    task: "Build the mobile app frontend. Read the expo SKILL.md, then implement the UI components and screens. Use the design tokens from constants/colors.ts via the useColors hook (colors and radius), configure fonts in app/_layout.tsx, and set the splash background in app.json.",
   246→    relevantFiles: [
   247→        ".local/skills/expo/SKILL.md",
   248→        "artifacts/my-app/app/_layout.tsx",
   249→        "artifacts/my-app/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx",
   250→        "artifacts/my-app/app/(tabs)/index.tsx",
   251→        "artifacts/api-server/src/index.ts",
   252→        "artifacts/my-app/constants/colors.ts",
   253→        "artifacts/my-app/hooks/useColors.ts",
   254→        "artifacts/my-app/app.json"
   255→    ]
   256→});
   257→
   258→await presentArtifact({artifactId: result.artifactId});
   259→await suggestDeploy();
   260→```
   261→
   262→### Dashboard scaffold with chart/table defaults (`data-visualization`)
   263→
   264→```javascript
   265→const result = await createArtifact({
   266→    artifactType: "data-visualization",
   267→    slug: "sales-dashboard",
   268→    previewPath: "/sales-dashboard/",

   280→    ]
   281→});

   283→await presentArtifact({artifactId: result.artifactId});
   284→await suggestDeploy();

   289→
   290→### Multiple artifacts in one workspace
   291→
   292→Each artifact must have a unique `slug` and `previewPath`. At least one artifact MUST use `previewPath: "/"` — otherwise users will see a blank page at the root.

   298→- Each `slug` can only be used once — calling `createArtifact` again with the same `slug` will fail

openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  # Do not change the title, if the title changes, the import paths will be broken
  title: Api
  version: 0.1.0
  description: Maritime Email Extraction API
servers:
  - url: /api
    description: Base API path
tags:
  - name: health
    description: Health operations
  - name: extraction
    description: Email extraction operations
  - name: history
    description: Extraction history
  - name: stats
    description: Cost and performance statistics
  - name: knowledge
    description: Maritime knowledge base
paths:
  /healthz:
    get:
      operationId: healthCheck
      tags: [health]
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/HealthStatus"

  /emails/extract:
    post:
      operationId: extractEmail
      tags: [extraction]
      summary: Extract structured data from a maritime email
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/ExtractionInput"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Extraction result
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ExtractionJob"
        "400":
          description: Bad request

  /emails/extract/json:
    post:
      operationId: extractEmailJson
      tags: [extraction]
      summary: Extract maritime email and return pure enterprise JSON array
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/ExtractionInput"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Enterprise JSON array of extracted entries
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: "#/components/schemas/EnterpriseEntry"
        "400":
          description: Bad request

  /emails:
    get:
      operationId: listExtractions
      tags: [history]
      summary: List past extraction jobs
      parameters:
        - name: limit
          in: query
          schema:
            type: integer
            default: 20
        - name: offset
          in: query
          schema:
            type: integer
            default: 0
        - name: emailType
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [VC, TC, Tonnage, Mixed, Unknown]
      responses:
        "200":
          description: List of extraction jobs
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ExtractionListResponse"

  /emails/{id}:
    get:
      operationId: getExtraction
      tags: [history]
      summary: Get a single extraction job
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: integer
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Extraction job details
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ExtractionJob"
        "404":
          description: Not found

    delete:
      operationId: deleteExtraction
      tags: [history]
      summary: Delete an extraction job
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: integer
      responses:
        "204":
          description: Deleted

  /stats:
    get:
      operationId: getStats
      tags: [stats]
      summary: Get cost savings and extraction statistics
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Platform statistics
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/PlatformStats"

  /knowledge/regions:
    get:
      operationId: listRegions
      tags: [knowledge]
      summary: List maritime region abbreviations
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Regions list
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: "#/components/schemas/KnowledgeEntry"

  /knowledge/ports:
    get:
      operationId: listPorts
      tags: [knowledge]
      summary: List known maritime ports
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Ports list
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: "#/components/schemas/KnowledgeEntry"

  /knowledge/vessel-sizes:
    get:
      operationId: listVesselSizes
      tags: [knowledge]
      summary: List vessel size classifications
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Vessel sizes
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: "#/components/schemas/VesselSizeEntry"

components:
  schemas:
    HealthStatus:
      type: object
      properties:
        status:
          type: string
      required:
        - status

    ExtractionInput:
      type: object
      required:
        - emailText
      properties:
        emailText:
          type: string
          minLength: 10
        subject:
          type: ["string", "null"]

    EnterpriseEntry:
      type: object
      required:
        - email_type
        - vessel_name
        - vessel_type
        - dwt
        - cargo
        - cargo_type
        - load_port
        - discharge_port
        - open_port
        - open_date
        - close_date
        - laycan_start
        - laycan_end
        - quantity
        - quantity_unit
        - load_rate
        - discharge_rate
        - commission
        - imo
        - grt
        - nrt
        - loa
        - beam
        - grain_capacity
        - restrictions
        - matching_region
        - confidence_score
      properties:
        email_type:
          type: string
        vessel_name:
          type: string
        vessel_type:
          type: string
        dwt:
          type: string
        cargo:
          type: string
        cargo_type:
          type: string
        load_port:
          type: string
        discharge_port:
          type: string
        open_port:
          type: string
        open_date:
          type: string
        close_date:
          type: string
        laycan_start:
          type: string
        laycan_end:
          type: string
        quantity:
          type: string
        quantity_unit:
          type: string
        load_rate:
          type: string
        discharge_rate:
          type: string
        commission:
          type: string
        imo:
          type: string
        grt:
          type: string
        nrt:
          type: string
        loa:
          type: string
        beam:
          type: string
        grain_capacity:
          type: string
        restrictions:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string
        matching_region:
          type: string
        confidence_score:
          type: number

    ExtractionJob:
      type: object
      required:
        - id
        - emailText
        - emailType
        - pipeline
        - extractedEntries
        - processingMs
        - llmUsed
        - estimatedCostUsd
        - createdAt
      properties:
        id:
          type: integer
        emailText:
          type: string
        subject:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        emailType:
          type: string
          enum: [VC, TC, Tonnage, Mixed, Unknown]
        pipeline:
          type: string
          enum: [rule-based, template, llm-fallback]
        confidence:
          type: number
        extractedEntries:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/ExtractedEntry"
        processingMs:
          type: integer
        llmUsed:
          type: boolean
        estimatedCostUsd:
          type: number
        createdAt:
          type: string

    ExtractedEntry:
      type: object
      required:
        - entryType
        - fields
        - confidence
        - extractionMethod
      properties:
        entryType:
          type: string
          enum: [VC, TC, Tonnage]
        confidence:
          type: number
        extractionMethod:
          type: string
          enum: [rule-based, template, llm-fallback]
        fields:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/ExtractedFields"

    ExtractedFields:
      type: object
      properties:
        email_type:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        cargo_name:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        account_name:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        cargo_type:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        tonnage_name:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        tonnage_type:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        min_size:
          type: ["number", "null"]
        max_size:
          type: ["number", "null"]
        region:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        matching_region:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        load_port:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        discharge_port:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        del_port:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        redel_port:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        port:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        open_date:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        close_date:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        laycan_start_date:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        laycan_end_date:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        duration:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        dwt:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        imo:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        grt:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        nrt:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        loa:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        beam:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        grain_capacity:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        load_rate:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        discharge_rate:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        commission:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        pic:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        email_id:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        phone_number:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        restriction:
          type: ["string", "null"]
        reason:
          type: ["string", "null"]

    ExtractionListResponse:
      type: object
      required:
        - items
        - total
      properties:
        items:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/ExtractionJob"
        total:
          type: integer

    PlatformStats:
      type: object
      required:
        - totalEmails
        - ruleBasedCount
        - templateCount
        - llmFallbackCount
        - estimatedLlmSavingsUsd
        - avgProcessingMs
        - avgConfidence
        - costReductionPct
        - recentActivity
      properties:
        totalEmails:
          type: integer
        ruleBasedCount:
          type: integer
        templateCount:
          type: integer
        llmFallbackCount:
          type: integer
        estimatedLlmSavingsUsd:
          type: number
        avgProcessingMs:
          type: number
        avgConfidence:
          type: number
        costReductionPct:
          type: number
        recentActivity:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/ActivityPoint"

    ActivityPoint:
      type: object
      required:
        - date

        - llmUsed
      properties:
        date:
          type: string
        count:
          type: integer
        llmUsed:
          type: integer

    KnowledgeEntry:
      type: object
      required:
